food_subgroup: Leavened flatbread with paneer filling, tandoor-baked

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for kulcha or tandoori paneer kulcha; closest parent 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_03315091) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Flat bread) is the closest parent; no specific kulcha or stuffed flatbread code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559003 (Flatbread) is the closest available parent concept; no specific kulcha or paneer kulcha term exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium tandoori paneer kulcha weighing approximately 150 g, typically served with chole (chickpea curry), mint chutney, or pickle.

nutrient_source.title: Kulcha, plain and stuffed variants — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) and USDA FoodData Central reference values for paneer and refined wheat flour
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN (2017) provides values for plain kulcha and paneer. Composite nutrient profile for tandoori paneer kulcha is estimated from ICMR-NIN plain kulcha base (approximately 55% refined wheat flour dough) combined with paneer filling (approximately 25% by weight) and ghee/butter finishing, consistent with standard North Indian restaurant preparation. Energy and macros cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entries for naan (NDB 42234) and paneer (NDB 01032) as secondary validation. Values represent a typical mixed-preparation estimate per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for naan/flatbread analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tandoori paneer kulcha. GI estimated at 57 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for naan (GI ~59, refined wheat flour base) and plain kulcha (GI ~55, leavened refined wheat flatbread). The paneer filling (high protein, high fat) moderates glycemic response relative to plain naan; a composite GI of 57 is defensible as a midpoint estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (57 × 39.5 × 1.50) / 100 = 33.9 g per 150 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~630 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a kulcha and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or salted chutneys, as a single serving contributes approximately 27% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and high glycemic load per serving (~34 g) due to refined wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should consume tandoori paneer kulcha in small portions (half piece, ~75 g) and pair it with high-fibre vegetables or legumes to blunt the postprandial glucose response; the paneer filling provides protein and fat that partially moderate glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and ghee (~4.8 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional servings, request minimal ghee finishing, and balance the meal with fibre-rich sides, as the saturated fat content (~7.2 g per 150 g serving) represents approximately 36% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit of 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori Paneer Kulcha is a popular North Indian leavened flatbread originating from the Punjab region of India. It is made from refined wheat flour (maida) dough that is stuffed with a spiced filling of crumbled paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) mixed with finely chopped onion, green chilli, fresh coriander, cumin, and tandoori spice blend. The stuffed dough is flattened and baked directly on the inner wall of a clay tandoor oven at very high heat (typically 400–480°C), producing a bread with a slightly charred, blistered exterior, a soft and chewy interior, and a rich smoky aroma. It is finished with a brushing of butter or ghee. The dish is widely served in Indian restaurants across Singapore and is commonly accompanied by chole (spiced chickpea curry), mint-coriander chutney, and sliced onion. Nutritionally, tandoori paneer kulcha provides a moderate energy density (~272 kcal per 100 g), meaningful protein from paneer (~9.2 g per 100 g), and calcium (~138 mg per 100 g). The refined wheat flour base gives it a medium glycemic index (~57), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g) and saturated fat from paneer and ghee (~4.8 g per 100 g) are relevant considerations for those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in paneer kulcha?
What is the GI of kulcha?

confidence_reason: No exact ICMR-NIN entry for tandoori paneer kulcha exists as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain kulcha and paneer entries with proportional composition modelling. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database naan and kulcha data. All source materials are from approved primary sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN (2017) plain kulcha and paneer entries combined in a ~55:25:20 ratio (dough:paneer filling:ghee/spices) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central naan and paneer entries. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this food category. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this food category. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for this food category. 5. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for tandoori paneer kulcha was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The input cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' appears to be a field survey platform tagging error; this dish contains no chicken and is a vegetarian paneer preparation. 7. food_group has been corrected from 'Meat and alternatives' (input) to 'Grains and staples' as kulcha is a flatbread product; paneer is a filling component, not the primary food group determinant. 8. Serving size of 150 g is based on typical restaurant portion for one medium kulcha piece.
